parsed_review.summary: CP00-349 is a descriptor-only RP11.P04 time-expense UI-binding-tail pack (10 RP11 units, RP11.P04.M05.S16-RP11.P04.M06.S05, risk A). Reviewed all 7 modified files plus src/index.js via Read/Grep/Glob. Pack binding (registry.js:127), requirements (registry.js:826), case set + descriptor (service.js:1098/1131), validators (validators.js:1565/1600), tests (model.test.js:498-546), validator script, contract current_pack/ui_binding_tail_descriptor, and README are mutually consistent: unit_count 10, micro split 5/5 (M05/M06), deliverable distribution matches the baseline, and the two descriptor sections cover all 10 planned row titles. Every row carries descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe_errors_only/raw_payload_included=false plus the M05/M06 no-leak guards (synthetic_fixture_binding.real_client_data_loaded=false, build_smoke.executes_ui_runtime=false, hermes_ui_evidence.emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, claude_ui_leak_prompt.leak_detected=false, empty_state.no_unauthorized_count_leak=true, denied_state.permission_decision_detail_included=false), and the validator enforces each. The CP00-348 P3 remediation is verified: no stale cp299-cp306 case_set_id labels remain anywhere in packages/time-expense; each pack's case set now carries its own number and references its upstream case_set_id. Boundaries hold: synthetic_only/no_real_data/no writes are attested in source (freezeCp349Result, CP349_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION) and enforced by validators; Hermes usage is a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (evidence_packet label H11.CP00-349, receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime, matching hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false. authority_boundary keeps Claude read-only and non-final-approver with no enterprise-trust claim from local validation (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false). index.js was unmodified but re-exports via export * so the new CP349 functions are exported (index_export_check holds). No over-claim: manifest production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false/implementation_layer=descriptor and only a descriptor-verified flag is asserted, which implementation/tests/local validation support (command-evidence shows npm test, validate, rp11/rp00/spec/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout validate, git diff --check all exit 0; H11 gate passed). Readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON (no auth failure, non-zero stdout, well-formed). One P3 (non-blocking): the manifest references three post-review evidence artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) plus npm run closeout-pack:validate that are pending by the documented hardening sequence (they are produced/run after this read-only claude run) and must complete before commit. Verdict: PASS - the read-only review accepts the descriptor-level claim; human final approval and the remaining post-review/closeout gates remain required and are correctly NOT pre-claimed by this pack.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts and closeout-pack:validate pending by documented hardening sequence
findings.details: The manifest required_pack_artifacts and per-unit evidence_refs reference docs/closeout-packs/cp00-349/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, which do not yet exist on disk (Glob of docs/closeout-packs/cp00-349/ returns only manifest.json and command-evidence.json). Likewise, npm run closeout-pack:validate is absent from command-evidence.json commands and is explicitly listed there as a post-review gated command. At this read-only-claude-run stage these artifacts/commands cannot yet exist, so this is not a pack defect; it is a downstream closeout precondition to track.
findings.recommendation: Before commit/closeout, ensure the remaining hardening steps complete and pass: receipt normalization, receipt validation, finding adjudication (adjudication.md), construction inspection (construction-inspection.json), and final closeout validation including npm run closeout-pack:validate and git diff --check --cached, plus the human final approval required for any enterprise-trust claim.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected by the manifest hardening_sequence (read_only_claude_run_raw_capture precedes finding_adjudication and construction_inspection). Non-blocking for this read-only review; recorded as a closeout-gate precondition, not a defect.
